title: Asset Type
description: Definition of an asset type. Assets are binary files (images, audio, video, etc.) that cannot be directly serialized into JSON. Assets are stored separately from artifacts and referenced via manifests.

id.description: Unique identifier for this asset type

mime_types.description: Allowed MIME types for this asset type (e.g., 'image/png', 'audio/mpeg')

manifest_fields.description: Additional metadata fields for the asset manifest beyond the standard fields (file_path, file_hash, mime_type, size_bytes)

default_store.description: Default store where assets of this type are persisted

processing_hints.description: Hints for how the runtime should handle this asset type

generate_thumbnails.description: For images/videos, whether to generate thumbnails

extract_metadata.description: Whether to extract embedded metadata (EXIF, ID3, etc.)

compute_dimensions.description: For images/videos, whether to compute width/height

compute_duration.description: For audio/video, whether to compute duration
